meta.title: Proxmox Network Commands — ip, ping, ss, pve-firewall, iptables | ProxMenux
meta.description: Reference of Proxmox VE host networking commands: ip / ip link / ip addr, ping, traceroute, mtr, ss, ethtool, dig, nslookup, plus pve-firewall, iptables and nftables for filtering. Bridge, bond and VLAN inspection.
meta.ogTitle: Proxmox Network Commands — ip, ping, ss, pve-firewall, iptables
meta.ogDescription: Reference of network commands for Proxmox VE — interface inspection, connectivity tests, monitoring, firewall (pve-firewall, iptables, nftables) and DNS lookups.
meta.twitterDescription: Network inspection, testing, configuration and firewall commands for Proxmox VE.

header.title: Network Commands
header.description: Curated reference for Proxmox host networking: interface info, connectivity tests, configuration, traffic monitoring and firewall management. Combines ip / ss / nmcli / ethtool / tcpdump / pve-firewall.

intro.title: Quick interface inventory
intro.body: ip a shows every interface with its IPs and MAC. ip r shows the routing table. Together they answer most "is this interface configured correctly?" questions in seconds.

commands.description: Combine ping and traceroute in real-time
Show network device details (if NetworkManager is used)
List open network files and connections
Compile firewall rules for all nodes

iptablesTip.title: iptables vs nftables
iptablesTip.bodyRich: Modern Debian / Proxmox uses nftables as the underlying firewall engine. The classic iptables -L -n -v command still works as a translation layer, but the source of truth is nft list ruleset. pve-firewall commands are Proxmox's wrapper that emits nftables rules from the cluster firewall config.

items.label: Network Management
items.tail: — ProxMenux interactive network tools (bridge analysis, persistent names, monitoring).
